It's at Oxenford near the Gold Coast theme parks, a place where homes usually sell within 30 days, but the unit she shares with boyfriend Wayne has been on the market six months without a nibble. Perhaps it has something to do with the fact she's been there seven years and hasn't done a thing by way of repairs and enhancements.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: When Lynne and Paul met while sailing five years ago, neither had plans to fall in love, let alone relocate to the wilds of Tasmania to build a home. But that all changes for the Melbourne couple when they visit the Apple Isle and find a beautiful stretch of coastal wilderness with panoramic views over the Tasman Sea. With grand plans to build a huge Modern home, they decide that Paul will project manage construction. But living onsite for months on end in a battered up old caravan will test his skills, endurance and ultimately his health.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Furniture maker Lucinda, son Dan and daughter Rosie build a wooden armadillo house. They need to find innovative ways to transport materials without damaging tree roots without heavy machinery.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Mary and Tom were looking to sell after Mary was diagnosed with a degenerative disease that affects her mobility. But after a year on the market, the couple need help so they can downsize to a more suitable home.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Property in the coastal town of Austinmer is so hot that buying another house before you sell is considered the norm. Judy and Graham Mahoney never dreamed they would have trouble selling their three-bedroom weatherboard cottage, so they went ahead and purchased a new home before they sold. After 10 months on the market, a failed auction, and 80 inspections, they have a big problem and an even bigger bridging loan. Buyers are turned off by the bad presentation, poor level of maintenance, and the lack of a communal bathroom. Despite the feedback, Judy and Graham refuse to budge from their $700,000 asking price. They need the money to renovate their new home, but as each month passes, the couple slip further into debt. They have loans on both properties and need to find almost $6000 each month just to stay afloat.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan and the team are creating a garden dedicated to the celebration of all things textural. Full of plants that you want to stroke, touch and feel, this garden is being created for a hardworking support worker.

programme.title: All Rise
programme.desc: A new courthouse drama that follows the chaotic, hopeful and sometimes absurd lives of its judges, prosecutors and public defenders, as they work with bailiffs, clerks and cops to get justice for the people of Los Angeles amidst a flawed legal process. Among those working within the court system is newly appointed Judge Lola Carmichael, a highly regarded and impressive deputy district attorney who doesn't intend to sit back on the bench in her new role, but instead leans in, immediately pushing the boundaries and challenging the expectations of what a judge can be. Those offering Lola counsel are her roguish best friend and colleague, Mark Callan, a successful deputy district attorney who now must adjust to the shifting boundaries in their personal and professional relationship; Supervisory Judge Lisa Benner, an influential, powerful figure tasked with overseeing Lola's transition into her new job; and Lola's efficient, pragmatic judicial assistant, Sherri Kansky.

programme.title: Blue Bloods

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Jonny Rayner faces a difficult choice as he decides whether to cross a crocodile-infested river or delay his journey.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, it's time for the Big Dogs: five trucks with 40-inch-plus tyres roll onto the course. But big rigs mean big damage. In the Green Hell, a rivalry heats up with a collision into a tree and a finalist lost in the woods.

programme.title: Superman & Lois
programme.desc: The Kents fight with everything they have as Luthor makes his final move on Smallville.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Jared and Johnny's epic mission grinds to a halt, Roscoe battles the elements on a world-famous rally, and Turbo is flagged down by the police.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man
programme.desc: An animated television show that focuses on a sixteen-year-old Peter Parker, and the origins of Spider-Man.

programme.title: Adventure Time: Fionna & Cake
programme.desc: Stranded in a desolate land, Fionna, Cake and Simon are stranded and resort to desperate measures to escape.

programme.title: Adventure Time: Fionna & Cake
programme.desc: Simon finds his mind transported across time and space while danger closes in on Fionna and Cake.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Christian racks up massive mileage with a huge load of cargo, Turbo faces chaos, and the Wise couple feel the pressure of a fuel-draining headwind.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

Ray meets Ally at the dog park and there is immediate chemistry - but he doesn't act on it, feeling that she's out of his league. Gary is in trouble with his Russian wife, Tatiana, over the discovery of a porn DVD and asks Ray to take the blame. While out with Brittany having ice-cream they bump into some young men who knock her ice-cream to the ground and disrespect Ray. Ray assaults one of the young men responsible and while escaping the scene he runs into Ally and takes the opportunity to make his feelings a little clearer. Ally gives Ray her number.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray kidnaps a man named Luke Henson and "takes care" of him out in a forest. Brittany questions her dad about Santa Claus and unicorns. The police have identified Ray as the young man's assailant; he informs Bruce that he has to go to court. Ray has his first date with Ally. It goes well. Freddy informs Ray that he was mistaken about Luke Henson. Ray is not happy about having killed the guy. Showing some moral compass and compassion Ray decides to pay Luke's widow an anonymous visit and give her some cash. Gary is in an induced coma after being bashed in a gun deal gone wrong set up by his brother-in-law, Vasilli. Vasilli's story doesn't add up and when confronted with a surprise visit by Ray, some of the truth comes out. Once Ray discovers who Gary's assailants were, he lets them know that they shouldn't mess with the people he cares about.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary wakes from his coma, and thanks Ray for doing "a bit of this and a bit of that" to Vassili and the guys who bashed him. Ray and Nick are becoming friends and he asks Nick for a favour. Jacinta berates Ray for telling Brittany that Jesus isn't real. The court has ordered Ray to attend anger management. His first meeting doesn't go very well. Freddy informs Ray that the guys he bashed for putting Gary in a coma belong to a crew run by his son-in-law's brother, Davros. Freddy insists Ray sits down with Davros to smooth the waters. Nick makes good on the favour. While on drugs, Vasilli breaks into Gary's house to rob the contents of his safe. Gary, who has just returned home from hospital, manages to send an SOS text to Ray who is out shopping with Ally. Ray comes to Gary's rescue and promptly deals with Vasilli - but they'll have to work out what to do with the body as Tatiana has arrived home unexpectedly.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary takes Ray for a drive to give him a surprise as a thank you for saving him. Ray and Ally's relationship has developed. Freddy takes Ray to meet Davros. Ray doesn't like Davros and although Davros seems placated by the end of the conference, Freddy knows better. A road-rage incident forces Ally to confront Ray about his quick access to violence, rather than attempting to diffuse a situation. When court-ordered anger management therapist Peter confronts Ray about the same impulse, Ray explains that it's justifiable to let people clearly know that there are ramifications for being an a******. For Ray, it's all about respect. Ray brings Ally home to meet Brittany. Ray can't sleep and gets up to notice a van parked outside surveilling his house. He goes for a drive and leads the van out to a safe place to do some permanent damage to it's occupants - Davros' guys - using the surprise Gary gave him.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Gary are planning to relieve a guy of his drugs and cash, and bring Nick in on the job, but a sudden turn of events scuppers their plans. After informing Freddy about his and Gary's plans going astray, Ray is picked up by Davros' slow-witted bodyguard, Bobby, and the wily hitman, Dave, who abduct Ray in his own car. Ray tells his captors about a hidden strongbox containing a great deal more cash than they're being paid to do the job. Dave devises a plan: tell Davros they ran out of gas, tell Ray that they'll let him go once the strongbox is retrieved, retrieve it, deliver Ray to Davros, and get paid twice. What could possibly go wrong? Dave and Bobby uncover the strongbox, but when they momentarily leave Ray's car unattended with Ray safely locked in the trunk, the car disappears. Dave and Bobby hot wire another car and go looking for Ray, the car, and the strongbox.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.sub-title: Your Mum's Got A Strong Box
programme.desc: We meet potheads, Scon and Lefty, and discover they are the reason for the disappearance of Ray and his car. Scon and Lefty drop off Ray's car to auto mechanic, Bernie, to be chopped for parts. Bernie is surprised to find Ray in the boot and has to dump Ray's car in a nearby forest with him still tied-up inside. Ray kicks his way out of the trunk, and shortly afterwards encounters Scon and Lefty with his phone and his strongbox. Trailing them back to Bernie's auto shop, Ray confronts the trio just as they're about to torch open his strongbox, which Ray reclaims intact. Dave and Bobby intercept Ray, and finally deliver him to Davros. As Ray prepares to be dispatched to his final resting place, Davros decides he wants in on the contents of the strongbox. Only when it's finally opened do Davros, Dave, and Bobby discover that it doesn't exactly contain what they were expecting, and are reminded that greed isn't necessarily good.

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: Lan Mao Shi Zai Wuding Shang
programme.desc: Walden's past day or so with Kate has him convinced she is the one he will spend the rest of his life with, until a stranger knocks on his door.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: An Expensive Glitch And A Goof-Off Room
programme.desc: Due to a four-hour gap between his classes, Sheldon is given his private dorm room. Running into fellow student Sam, she says he gets undeserved special treatment, and nobody likes him because of his arrogance. Talking with Dr. Sturgis about humans being social creatures, Sheldon decides to experiment to see if people will like him more. After letting Sam use the room to study, word spreads Sheldon has a free room. He allows couples to use it to study, unaware of why they want it. When his parents find out, they tell him people are using it to waste time, convincing Sheldon to stop lending it out. Meanwhile, a glitch in the slot machines lets June win $11,000. Unable to pay it, Connie is forced to let June become a partner in her casino business. June and Georgie support each other's ideas for the place. Connie tries to talk Georgie to her side, but he says Connie hates anything she doesn't think of herself. Georgie adds a small disco ball, further angering Connie.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Lot Of Band-Aids And The Cooper Surrender
programme.desc: Sheldon meets Oscar and Darren, undergrads in the dorm room next door to his. Finding they have many things in common like video games, Sheldon likes hanging out with them. Meanwhile, George's job is at risk due to parents who are unhappy with the football team's performance. George's meeting with them doesn't go well. A stressed-out George lets Sheldon stay overnight to play Dungeons & Dragons at the dorm without consulting Mary. Though annoyed, Mary is able to talk to George and give him a sincere hug when she learns what is bothering him. Mary later goes to get Sheldon herself, as he is about to vomit from too much candy and root beer. Also, after clothes shopping, Missy is made fun of at school and accidentally injures herself trying to shave her legs. Connie patches her up and promises to show her how to do it safely, cheering Missy up.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Lainey surprises everyone with her college announcement, prompting Barry to vow to follow in the footsteps of Doogie Howser MD and become a doctor while still in high school.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tells a lie that lands him a prom date with an older classmate. Barry asks Erica to help make the dance a night to remember for him and Lainey.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake thinks Boyle might have met his soulmate in Genevieve, an art gallery owner, and he tries to help set them up. Meanwhile, Rosa deals with inter-precinct theft and Holt seeks Amy's help with an image campaign.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When investigating a serial killer, Jake enlists Holt's help in an attempt to solve the case off the radar. At the precinct, Rosa and Amy are forced to deal with demands from an old adversary and Terry discovers a new obsession.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur accidentally starts a fire in Doug and Carrie's basement, so Carrie then begins to think that her and Doug should find him a retirement home to go live in. When Doug starts to clean out the basement by removing Arthur's things, Doug sees that Arthur used to be an actor and he finds out that he gave up the career for Carrie. This gives Doug second thoughts on giving Arthur the boot.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Pink Cadillac And A Glorious Tribal Dance
programme.desc: Mary runs into Mr. Lundy who has a pink Cadillac from selling Mary Kay makeup products. He talks Mary into selling for him. She initially gets one sale to the high school librarian Ms. Hutchins. Mr. Lundy talks Mary into selling to the women in her Bible Study group. Mary succeeds, but later has a guilt-ridden dream for doing so, and quits being a seller. Sheldon is on Spring Break from college when he hears about a three-day comic convention. Unable to get an adult to go with him, Sheldon decides to rebel and take a bus to the convention. Seeing someone from his local comic shop and his old science teacher Mr. Givens, they debate the merits of Lost in Space, Star Trek and The Next Generation. Sheldon enjoys the trip though gets in trouble afterwards for sneaking out.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Grace learns that an old school mate who she made fun of has died, guilt makes her attend the funeral. Meanwhile, Jack is nominated for a gay cabaret award.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Heat 6 - Shirley Ballas, Nicky Spence, Josh Widdicombe
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Explore Australia's sporting fortresses, delve into stories of their distinctive, sometimes unplayable wickets, antagonistic crowds and harsh climates. Meet the iconic batsmen and bowlers, heroic captains, and observe on-field antics ranging from poor behaviour to instances of brilliance and sportsmanship.

programme.title: Dirty Driving: Thundercars of Indiana
programme.desc: Auto racing is an obsession in Anderson, Indiana. Even with local auto factories closing down, the town's residents continue to flock to the local speedway every Friday night.

programme.title: Back On The Record With Bob Costas
programme.desc: Interviews with the biggest names in sports, entertainment and popular culture, and discussions led by Bob Costas that will address today's most topical sports issues.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo learns about the Victorian fascination with Britain's own Atlantis to the lost city of Dunwich, meets some gentle giants who were crucial to the smooth running of the railways and discovers how the Port of Felixstowe grew into the biggest container port in the country.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: A look at the composers who dominated the era that gave us films like ET The Extra-Terrestrial, The Terminator and The Shining.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: With the help of archaeologists and scientists, this series looks to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the world's most fascinating treasures, shining a light on Ancient History and playing into people's thirst for conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Six semi-finalists have been chosen by judges Tai Shan Schierenberg, Kate Bryan and Kathleen Soriano, and now they must paint the otherworldly view of dormant oil rigs that dominate the horizon. Joining them in their all-weather pods is this year's Wildcard Winner, bringing, the total to seven extremely talented but very different artists all competing for a place in the final and a shot at the prize, a 10,000 commission to create a landscape vista of Venice for the prestigious Royal Institute of British Architects. With only 3 places available in the final, who has what it takes to become Sky Arts Landscape Artist of the Year?

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael comes face-to-face with a medieval politician, takes a rail tour of Victorian freak show hotspots, and visits Southend to ride one of the world's first electric railways.

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: His performances in the West End, as well as in classic films like Scrooge, earned him widespread fame. Barry Cryer looks back on the fruitful career of Alastair Sim.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: A look at the composers who dominated the era that gave us films like ET The Extra-Terrestrial, The Terminator and The Shining.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Explore Australia's sporting fortresses, delve into stories of their distinctive, sometimes unplayable wickets, antagonistic crowds and harsh climates. Meet the iconic batsmen and bowlers, heroic captains, and observe on-field antics ranging from poor behaviour to instances of brilliance and sportsmanship.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo visits Essex to discover why dairy herds travelled there by rail from all over the country in the 19th century. He also visits Waltham Cross to see how the gunpowder made there fuelled the building of an empire and heads to Hackney to uncover the gruesome details of the first murder on a train.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo takes a ride on a secret miniature railway hidden beneath London's streets, rings the bells of Bow Church, and tries his hand at station announcing at Fenchurch Street station.

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: Barry Cryer celebrates the career of the Carry On, Doctor in the House and The Navy Lark star.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: In the mid-80s, films like Back to the Future, Die Hard and The Abyss dominated cinema, and four composers took centre stage.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: With the help of archaeologists and scientists, this series looks to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the world's most fascinating treasures, shining a light on Ancient History and playing into people's thirst for conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Hundreds of artists from all over the UK and Ireland have battled with brushes, rollers, paints and pencils for just 3 places in the final. As the sun sets over London, our three finalists face their toughest challenge yet to create a night-painting of this iconic landmark that will see judges Tai Shan Schierenberg, Kate Bryan and Kathleen Soriano crown one of them the Landscape Artist of the Year 2019.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Heat 7 - Joe Sugg, Daryl McCormack, Lenny Rush
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Steve Jobs: The Lost Interview
programme.desc: In this candid, in-depth interview with the late visionary filmed in 1995, Steve Jobs discusses at length his early days, career battles, and vision for the future. Small portions of the piece were used for a television series at the time, but the vast majority was shelved and for 17 years thought to be lost. Resurfacing, it is being presented in its entirety, providing a fascinating look at Jobs at a particularly interesting moment in his career, two years before he would go on to retake control of Apple.

programme.title: My Name Is Gulpilil
programme.desc: "I'm an actor, I'm a dancer, I'm a singer and also, a painter. This film is about me. This is my story of my story". David Gulpilil is an iconic figure of Australian cinema and has been for fifty years. His mesmerising, electrifying presence has leapt off the big screen and changed Australian screen representation forever.

programme.title: The Wonder List with Bill Weir
programme.desc: Conservationists turn killers in this battle to turn back time by eradicating mammal invaders threatening the islands' unique ecosystem.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A look at the Britpop band's most famous videos, including Parklife, featuring Phil Daniels, Country House, directed by Damien Hirst, and Coffee & TV.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Human League singer Phil Oakey and director Steve Barron reflect on the iconic videos of the new wave band, including that of hit single Don't You Want Me.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Mumford & Sons is a British folk rock band formed in London in 2007; Hozier is, as the song goes, a man you don't meet every day.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Rufus McGarrigle Wainwright is an American-Canadian musician. John Grant established himself as one of the great musicalchroniclers of the American Dream, angled mostly from its flipside.

programme.title: Canada's National Parks
programme.desc: Canada is the second largest country in the world and historically the first to recognize the need to protect the diversity of its landscapes and preserve them for future generations. The over 100-year-old concept of Canadian National Parks is instrumental in protecting the unique natural environment and habitats of native flora and fauna from human influence. This is a challenging task for the employees of Parks Canada, as they have to reconcile tourism and economic interests with nature. And then, of course, there is climate change. Whether and how these developments are putting a strain on sensitive ecosystems will be the main topic of the discovery series, which brings Canada's unique landscape and culture to life. Knowledgeable and charismatic protagonists, including scientists, First Nations people, environmental activists, artists and storytellers who have a special connection with the landscape guide the viewer through this journey.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present, pointing to the quirky, the surprising and the beautiful lying just offshore.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: In July 2012, cousins Lyric Cook and Elizabeth Collins are abducted, devastating their families. Suspicion falls on one family before hunters discover the girls' bodies, deepening the mystery.

programme.title: Casey Anthony: Where the Truth Lies
programme.desc: Caylee Anthony went missing in 2008, but her mother didn't tell anyone for a month. Now for the first time ever, Casey Anthony is opening up, asking only to be heard.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors discuss working with Aretha Franklin, including the difficulties experienced making the video for her duet with George Michael.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Ryan Adams has been roundly recognised as one of the most talented songwriters of his generation; Glen Hansard is an Irish singer-songwriter.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael looks at his second travel series, Pole to Pole (1992), and how it came about.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present, pointing to the quirky, the surprising and the beautiful lying just offshore.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: In July 2012, cousins Lyric Cook and Elizabeth Collins are abducted, devastating their families. Suspicion falls on one family before hunters discover the girls' bodies, deepening the mystery.

programme.title: Casey Anthony: Where the Truth Lies
programme.desc: Caylee Anthony went missing in 2008, but her mother didn't tell anyone for a month. Now for the first time ever, Casey Anthony is opening up, asking only to be heard.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo visits a station fit for royalty in Windsor, views the Maidenhead Railway Bridge an engineering triumph built by Isambard Kingdom Brunel to span the River Thames at Maidenhead, and tries his hand at collecting the mail 'Victorian style' on a steam-powered travelling post office.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo tastes a Victorian superfood in Alton, explores the fascinating Whitchurch silk mill, untouched for over 150 years, and tries his hand at driving a steam train on the challenging Watercress Line.

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: From Saturday Night Live to Hollywood superstardom, Barry Cryer looks back on the career of the actor and comedian who stars in Beverly Hills Cop, Dr Dolittle, The Nutty Professor and more.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: In the era of hit movies such as Titanic, Braveheart and Saving Private Ryan, a host of new composers began to emerge.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: With the help of archaeologists and scientists, this series looks to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the world's most fascinating treasures, shining a light on Ancient History and playing into people's thirst for conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: To celebrate the bicentenary of the birth of John Ruskin, our winning artist will visit some of this Victorian art critic's most-loved Venetian architectural icons, to photograph, sketch and take artistic inspiration, before returning to the UK to create a landscape artwork that will be unveiled by Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell, and hang at RIBA's prestigious London HQ.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: The World According To Amazon
programme.desc: An exploration into Amazon's business models and practices, from its inception to present day.

programme.title: Food, Inc. America's Food Industry
programme.desc: An unflattering look inside America's corporate controlled food industry. The current method of raw food production is largely a response to the growth of the fast food industry since the 1950s. The production of food overall has more drastically changed since that time than the several thousand years prior.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Leeds, at an enormous mattress factory. He follows the production of pocket-sprung mattresses from the arrival of hard steel right through to soft bedding heading out of dispatch. Along the way he learns how lengths of metal are stretched into thin wire and coiled into springs which are placed into individual pockets. And how the mattresses are designed to wick sweat away from our bodies with the help of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey is learning whether there are benefits to be had from taking an afternoon nap. She meets up with a sleep scientist who tells her that we should be making time for a snooze rather than reaching for a cup of coffee. To prove his point, he runs an experiment which demonstrates that reaction times can be improved by a short sleep. 20 minutes is the optimum nap time - any longer risks falling in to a deep sleep which is difficult to wake from.

programme.desc: This episode it's carb overload, with the remaining bakers taking on a signature challenge of savoury flat breads, a technical challenge of knotted bread rolls and a showstopper stollen bread. Who will rise to the challenge?

programme.desc: Gino travels through Puglia's meat country, taking in the town of Cisternino and the Itria valley.

Next up, cheeky berry meringue ripple, a ridiculously easy, emergency pud, and to top it all off, the fastest roast chicken ever, Jamie's harissa chicken traybake- a deliciously easy food that everyone's going to fight over.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Lancashire cake make Molly, turns to the silver screen to bring a touch of glamour to a 100th birthday celebration. Rosie also takes a trip to the movies for her stunning Bollywood Dancer cake. Containing 13 kilos of chocolate orange cake and 30 kilos of fondant, the masterpiece takes a whooping 6 days to make. Eloise heads west with a Canadian Teepee Wedding Cake. Standing 3 feet high, she creates a unique animal hide style fondant tent that will go around the cake, covered in hand painted native North American designs.

programme.title: Inside The Factory Christmas Special
programme.desc: Chocolates are an essential part of many people's festive celebrations. In this Christmas special, Gregg Wallace visits a factory that produces a staggering 2 million tins of festive chocolate assortments a year.

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's day two of finals week and the remaining bakers are on a mission to impress. They must fuse two delicious street food items in the technical before creating supersize snacks.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: All the dishes have a bit of spice, even the pudding. Jeremy starts with mouth-watering Sichuanese chicken, a warming dish using Sichuan peppercorns to create a unique numbing.

programme.title: Justine Schofield Queensland Gourmet
programme.desc: Justine heads to a local turmeric farm and forages for ingredients, and Terri Taylor prepares a batch of turmeric chicken skewers. Up in Port Douglas, Justine cooks up some kangaroo.

programme.title: Justine Schofield Queensland Gourmet
programme.desc: Justine visits the Spanish hideaway in the heart of Queensland, Paronella Park, and makes paella. Back on Whitehaven Beach, she grills up some mango cheeks.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Michael will be making his crackling pork belly. Courtney will be making her parsnip and cardamom cake with a healthy twist! Courtney and Andy will then head out to the broccoli farm.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Andy teams up with Darren Robertson to create a delectable dish of fried eggs and greens. Courtney prepares her flavourful brussels sprout slaw, accompanied by a Green Goddess tahini yogurt dressing.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: It's 7th week on the road Matt travels to the country music capital in Australia, Tamworth. This region known as the New England North West and has a special place in his heart because this is where he was born. Matt spent his first five years living in this region, more on that later. His first day in the area and Matt meets up with Doug Cush a wheat farmer who specialises in durum wheat the key ingredient in pasta. Doug flies Matt over the plains and fields of crop from Narrabri airport to his home in Bellata - a place world famous for its produce that even Italian past making companies knows about it. And Matt soon finds out that for the last 25 years he's been making his pasta all wrong. The next day he heads back towards Tamworth to the Dungowen Valley to find a chef with a difference. Graham Manvell and his wife Marlene show Matt their zoo of unique farm animals all bred with the purpose of going on the plates of their restaurants. But their most unique animal is the herd of Asian & Italian Water Buffalo that roam the valley. This captures Matt's attention and he cooks an old favourite with a buffalo twist - Buffalo Wellington. Not far from the Buffalo farm is Matt's next stop - Peel Valley Milk. Owner & dairy farmer Malcolm is a rebel in the dairy world deciding to build and run his own dairy factory to get his milk straight from the paddock to the hands and mouths of consumers. This is milk like it used to taste and it brings back memories for Matt's about his early years on his family dairy farm. Matt decides to track down his old family farm in Currububula. He left when he was five years old but the memories come flooding back as he drives back up the road to Glenbrooke Farm. An emotional Matt meets the current owner Karen Arnott and some old family friends who shed some light on him as a child. Matt's been asked to cook up a festive feast for the Tamworth council social club so he goes in search of the best Turkeys he can find in the region.

programme.desc: A Christmas baking celebration with six New Zealand celebrities coming in to compete for their charities and the opportunity to win $10,000 for their charity.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his speedy method for the perfect roast lamb with salsa verde and minty potatoes. For dessert, he makes an apricot pie with almond crumble.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: Gregg heads to the German capital for a 3 day trip. He gets to the heart of Berlin's food scene and gets involved in the beer culture. Between meals he explores the city's often turbulent past and indulges his love of the musical Cabaret!

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James encourages everyone to experiment in the kitchen and makes amazing comfort food from everyday larder ingredients. He is joined by special guest Cyrus Todiwala.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi-award-winning chef Brian Turner.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc rustles up simple, delicious dishes in his Oxfordshire kitchen.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in France at an enormous foundry where they produce a cast iron pot every five seconds. He follows production of casserole dishes, from the arrival of 20 tonnes of crude iron right through to brightly coloured orange pots. Along the way he tests his mettle by taking a sample of molten iron at 1,550 degree Celsius. With only a heatproof visor and gloves as protection he dips a ladle in to a bubbling cauldron and pours the white-hot sample into a tiny mould. He also discovers that the coloured enamel they protect their pots with is made from glass.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Wigan, at an enormous soup factory where they produce 2 million tins a day. He follows the production of vegetable soup, from a pea harvest in Yorkshire right through to the finished soup going into cans and being dispatched. Along the way he watches as a 5 tonne avalanche of peas, around 7 million individual peas, are frozen within 2 hours of being picked. He mixes up 3 tonnes of veg and 500 gallons of tomato sauce and watches as they're combined and packed into 10 000 tins. He's astonished by a 27.4 metre tall pressure cooking tower and surprised to learn that for every 1 degree drop in temperature in winter the factory observe a 3.5 percent increase in soup sales.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Ireland, at an enormous liqueurs factory where they produce 540,000 bottles a day. He follows the production of cream liqueur, from the arrival of maize to make Irish Whiskey, right through to dispatch of the finished liqueur. It's our longest ever production timeline, taking more than 3 years. Along the way Gregg learns that it's the barrels whiskey is matured in that create around half of its flavour and discovers that a milk protein is the secret to mixing cream and whiskey together.

programme.desc: Robert and Frances undergo couples therapy before Frances arrives at an epiphany.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Frances and Robert meet with a mediator; Frances gets emotional at work; Robert despairs over finances.

programme.title: Divorce

programme.title: Divorce

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: After finally signing divorce papers, a financially strapped Robert looks for work and a new living situation, while Frances battles insomnia and her newly complicated relationship with her children. programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Frances is forced to deal with Lila's growing resentment toward her. Diane blindsides Frances with a set-up, and Dallas attempts to call things off with Tony Silvercreek.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: After helping new friend Andrew appraise some art, an inspired Frances tries to track down an unknown painter. Diane tries to ignore her issues with Nick by throwing herself into the gallery. Sparks fly with a realtor when Robert shows his house to her.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: When Robert's dad falls ill, Robert and Frances reunite in Ohio, where they reminisce about happier times, and deal with Robert's overbearing sister, Cathy. Back in Hastings-on-Hudson, Tom jumps at the chance to chauffeur Diane around town in her luxury car.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Frances learns that Robert has a new woman in his life, and enlists Diane and Dallas to get intel on her before Lila's 13th birthday party.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: After Robert finds Tom in a compromising position, Frances and Robert revisit their marriage counsellor to set new ground rules for the kids. programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Frances spots the dashing art bigwig Skip Zakarian at an auction, and takes the opportunity to invite him to Sylvia's upcoming show, where he shows interest in more than art. programme.desc: Frances and Robert, having both moved on, discover that their new lives are much more complicated than anticipated.

programme.desc: While on tour, Shawna and Mia struggle to see eye to see with Reina Reign and each other until an uncomfortable afterpart places them back on the same team.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: Mia flies out to Atlanta to enjoy some down time with her latest fling. Meanwhile, Stanley gives Maurice an ultimatum, and Chastity and Shawna open up to each other despite a series of unfortunate events.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: Increasingly frustrated with Francois and Reina Reign's behaviour, Shawna, Mia, and Chastity start to push back. Later, Shawna bonds with Lord AK in the studio while Mia frets over her mistake with Cash.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: In LA, Francois entices Shawna and Mia with lucrative deals but only under one condition. At the Billboard party, Mia balances two love interests, Shawna and Lord AK draw closer together.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: Shaken up after their time in LA, Shawna and Mia finally return home to Miami. While Shawna reconnects with Maurice, Mia struggles to adjust to normal life, and Chastity tries to reintegrate herself back into work.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: With their tour officially over, Shawna weighs a career changing offer from Francois, while Chastity reaches back out to an A&R rep.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: When presented with differing opportunities, Shawna and Mia must decide once and for all whether their loyalties lie with Francois or Chastity.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: Before heading to her first ever live performance, Shawna faces off against a former friend, whose critical words about the pair's lack of direction hit home.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t
programme.desc: After the show, Mia and Shawna find themselves questioning the group's future. Later, Mia forms an unexpected connection, while Shawna considers an offer.

programme.title: Rap Sh!t

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: An extraordinary tale of a mother's love for her daughter, following the life of Costello Jones who wants more for her precocious young daughter.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: As Costello works her job at a local peep show, a journalist offers an exciting opportunity. Gloria unexpectedly forms a connection at the funeral home, while Selby meanders back to his old ways.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Missing Screenplay
programme.desc: At a New York film-society party, George introduces Jonathan to filmmaker Jim Jarmusch, a fan of Jonathan's first book, who is interested in having him rewrite a screenplay. Later, after leaving his personalised script in a therapist's office during an ill-advised late-night tryst with a very young party girl, Jonathan convinces Ray to pose as a patient in order to retrieve it. Meanwhile, George is flummoxed when he runs into his sexy ex-wife Priscilla and her arrogant new husband, fellow publishing magnate Richard Antrem.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Stolen Skateboard
programme.desc: Jonathan falls for his latest client, the mother of a boy whose skateboard was stolen by a neighbourhood bully. Meanwhile, Ray agrees to help a lesbian couple start a new family, to his wife Leah's chagrin. And George has a nostalgic 'armpit crush' on a young publicist he hopes to woo at the opening of a new Brooklyn restaurant.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Lonely White Dove
programme.desc: A smitten Russian parolee contracts Jonathan to track down a chanteuse known as 'the Lonely White Dove'. After a vodka-fuelled reconnaissance mission at the Brighton Beach restaurant where the singer works, Jonathan persuades Suzanne, along with Ray and Leah, to join him there for dinner the next night, without revealing his true purpose. Meanwhile, George decides to get in touch with his feminine side as a way to drive sales of his sagging magazines.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Beautiful Blackmailer
programme.desc: Leah hires Jonathan to help find her new dog, 'Little Ray', and Ray joins him in the search, getting some help from a local woman. A reflective George heads to the hospital for surgery.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Stolen Sperm
programme.desc: Concerned over the sudden disappearance of the two lesbians who've been buying his sperm, Ray enlists Jonathan to help track the couple down. Breaking into their apartment, Jonathan learns the pair has flown the coop - but not without leaving behind a clue that both shocks and intrigues Ray. Meanwhile, George ignores Jonathan's warnings by publishing a disparaging editorial about Richard Antrem, sending his publishing rival into a fit of rage at a local bar.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Having picked up the gauntlet thrown down by Richard - and having thwarted a blackmail bid aimed at getting Jonathan to take a dive - the Edition team gear up for a triple-header boxing challenge against their GQ rivals. But with the bouts only hours away, the women in their lives offer some compelling reasons why winning isn't everything.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: When we last dropped in on New York writer, romantic, unlicensed private detective Jonathan Ames he was taking a pounding in the boxing ring while still licking his breakup wounds. Now that his second novel has been completed (and rejected), Jonathan is augmenting his income as a creative-writing teacher, while taking on some new, desperate clients, ranging from a kinky policeman to the publishing rival of his sexagenarian pal, George Christopher (Ted Danson). How he solves anything is a mystery. In Season 2, Jason Schwartzman returns as Jonathan, who has also decided to be a teacher in a night class in creative writing, though few of his students have any idea who he is. He also continues to hang out with (and enlist the help of) his pal, a laconic, hen-pecked cartoonist named Ray (Zach Galifianakis). This season's guests include Oliver Platt, Laila Robins, John Hodgman and Kristen Wiig, all of whom appeared in Season 1. Other guests include Jessica Hecht, F. Murray Abraham, Kristen Johnston, Mary Kay Place, Kevin Bacon, Olympia Dukakis, John Rothman, Domenick Lombardozz, Richard Masur, Allyce Beasley, Finnerty Steeves, Joel Marsh Garland (The Bounty Hunter), Zoe Kazan (It's Complicated) and Jonathan Ames himself.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan is hired by Richard Antrem, George's nemesis and boxing foe, to investigate Richard's suspicions that his wife Priscilla (George's ex) is having an affair, and Jonathan's insecurities resurface when Stella announces she's not over her first love. George learns he has a new female urologist, while Ray plots to win back Leah by penning a comic book.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: George worries about remaining 'intact' after getting some sobering news from his new urologist, Dr Kenwood, and a bad day for Jonathan gets worse when a pair of S&M henchmen kidnap him, demanding a ransom that calls 'GI' George and Ray to action.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: I've Been Living Like A Demented God!
programme.desc: A professor hires Jonathan to track down a valuable item, while George finds an alibi to explain the results of his drug test. Flushed with cash from his Super Ray comic book, Ray heads to the bar and meets a familiar barfly.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan attempts to repair the relationship between a poet/limousine driver and his wife, and Ray has a meeting with Kevin Bacon.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Grievous Clerical Error!
programme.desc: Leah hires Jonathan to help find her new dog, Little Ray. Ray joins Jonathan in the search for the dog, getting some inside help from a local woman. A reflective George heads to the hospital for surgery.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Jonathan meets a new client, a dermatologist who asks him to deliver a letter to his intended at an Asian health spa. Jonathan decides to go with George and Ray to celebrate Ray's birthday, which ends up hurting Ray's feelings. George tries to cheer Ray up in the usual manner. At his writing class, Jonathan learns that Louis Greene won a contest with a story based on Jonathan. When Nina comforts him after class, things go a bit further than Jonathan expected.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After spending the night with Jonathan, Nina announces she wants to move in, but Ray has a crisis so Jonathan rushes over to his place before he and Nina can resolve the issue. Ray's stalker has sent him emails and a voodoo doll. Jonathan enlists the help of Officer Drake to find Ray's stalker. They convince Drake to track the stalker's emails to a specific computer. At Edition, Kathryn informs George that since he doesn't have cancer, he must be re-evaluated by their substance abuse experts. George prefers a sanitarium, but can he handle two months in Arizona?

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After celebrating the release of his new book The Blonde in the Woods, Jonathan learns some distressing familial news. George reunites with his daughter Emily and meets her new boyfriend. Ray spends time with his biological son Spencer. Meanwhile, Jonathan's latest case leaves him in a precarious position.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: On the run, Jonathan and Ray try to hunt down the person who framed Jonathan. Meanwhile, George covers for them despite his growing cannabis-induced paranoia.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: As a guest on The New Dick Cavett Show, Jonathan runs into his nemesis. George is surprised to hear Bernard and Emily's news, and Ray sets off an AMBER Alert.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: While showing Emily a 'youthful' time, Jonathan discovers a classified ad for a detective impersonating himself. On a night out with Bernard, George learns that Richard Antrem has opened a restaurant aimed at hurting George's restaurant. Ray, in hot water with Leah, finds himself drawn to a lonely woman named Belinda.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: I Keep Taking Baths Like Lady Macbeth
programme.desc: Jonathan and George see a 'friendship' counsellor and George begins taking singing lessons from a delightful pothead named Josephine. Jonathan attempts to win back George's affection and Ray becomes irresistible to women.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: Two Large Pearls And A Bar Of Gold
programme.desc: Jonathan's college sweetheart enlists him to guard her wedding jewels and reminds him of their prenuptial pact. Ray moves in with George, and George warns Josephine about growing too attached.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: Rose Hiney connects with Jonathan on some new leads. George and Ray attempt to right their unrightable wrongs.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.sub-title: Nothing I Can't Handle By Running Away
programme.desc: George throws a wedding for Emily and Bernard; Jonathan is hesitant to break it off with his new lady; Ray retreats into Spencer's arms; Jonathan gets taken hostage; Ray gathers an army of his new-found Super Ray fans.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: After his girlfriend Suzanne moves out because of his failure to stop drinking and smoking pot, between-books novelist Jonathan Ames peruses his favourite Raymond Chandler novel, Farewell, My Lovely, and is inspired to place an ad on Craigslist offering his services as an unlicensed private investigator. Within hours he gets his first case, involving a 19-year-old girl's desperate search for her missing sister. Despite the fact that he should be working on his second novel, and despite the fact that he is on deadline for a magazine piece, Jonathan heads out into the urban night to track down the girl, embracing his new-found noir-detective status. At the end of a very long day, Jonathan earns his fee - and finds he's got another case.

programme.title: Bored to Death
programme.desc: During an unsuccessful attempt to win back Suzanne, Jonathan gets a phone call and a new case: a woman named Jennifer wants him to tail her boyfriend Gary to see if he's been unfaithful. After learning that he and Jennifer share a common pastime - drinking - Jonathan goes in search of her boyfriend, with uncomfortable results. Meanwhile, Ray brings Jonathan along to his colonic for moral support, and George hatches a knockout plan for Jonathan to help him cover up an unsightly sore before a big speech.

programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Missing Screenplay
